# Transcrição: 02-Charlie s Shovel

- **Fonte:** 02-Charlie s Shovel
- **Duração:** 07:19
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:02]

I look up and I see coming across the sand there's a man. He's walking purposefully towards me and instantly I'm angry at my wife. I'm angry at my wife because I can tell that this man knows me and I don't know who he is because I have a wife who goes through the world saying hello to strangers. I'm talking to them constantly having deep and meaningful conversations with them in places all over and before I know I'm having brunch with them and then dinner and then two months later I'm at their parents retirement dinner and I still don't know who the fuck they are but my wife knows everything about them. And I run into these situations all the time and I can tell this guy knows me and he's coming to talk to me and I don't know who he is. And so I stand up and I get ready with the how you doing guy or what's up bro how you doing dude and I extend my hand and as I extend my hand to this guy his hand passes by mine. It slides right past mine and reaches down my son is at my feet he's playing in the sand or on a beach with buckets and shovels. This man reaches down and he grabs one of the shovels next to my son and he picks it up and he says this is mine. He doesn't say, excuse me sir, this shovel perhaps could be mine could we have a conversation about it or maybe my beach paraphernalia has migrated into your beach paraphernalia. He says this is mine. And I suddenly realized that this has nothing to do with my wife that I don't know this guy at all and he doesn't know me. He's a douchebag. I didn't recognize it when he approached me but he is 100% douchebag I'm looking at him now he's got a bathing suit on it's short and tight it's the kind of bathing suit that tells me he's far more comfortable with his situation that I am. He's the kind of guy who like makes jokes about sexual assault and then says it's locker room talk. You know he's got a body built from Peloton and kale and teeth whitener I can just tell he is an awful human being and now he's holding my son's shovel. And I am pissed and this is great for me because this is the perfect moment for me because my one superpower in the world is I know how to say the absolute worst thing at the right moment to make people feel terrible about themselves. And I am ready to lay into this guy, but my son Charlie who's five is at my feet. And all of the things that I want to say to this man now I can't because my son is here and now again I'm angry at my wife because I can see her. She's 100 yards down the beach and I swear she is talking to a stranger who will later be at my house for brunch but right now I am saddled with the child and I just want to lay into this guy. I want to do all the things that I do to make strangers who are terrible feel terrible and my wife hates it she can't stand it because she thinks that every time I do this I'm in public and I'm making someone cry that they're going to pull a gun or a knife but this guy is barely wearing anything.

## [03:02 - 06:09]

There is nothing on him that is going to bother me in any way it is the perfect situation for me to crush him and my son is here. And the thing I want to do the thing I want to do is I want to grab the shovel. I just want to put my hands on the shovel because if I do I know he's going to pull it away and the rule is the person who is yanking something out of someone else's hand that is the crazy person and if you turn someone into the crazy person in the midst of your fight you win every single time. But I can't do that because if I reach out and I grab the shovel things might happen like he might hit me which would be amazing because I love punching douchebags it's the best thing and you don't get to do it very often like once a decade you get to hit somebody and I can't do it because my son is here. So I have to say this pathetic thing this most sad and weak thing I say no I think it's our shovel and I just can't stand myself when I say it. And he says no it's my shovel and I just can't believe he's standing his ground like this for a $7 shovel and I just have so many things I want to say to him but I look down and Charlie's playing in the sand and he's so cute so I back off. I blunt all of the sharp things I want to say and I just say I know you have the shovel and I know you say you own the shovel but that doesn't actually mean it's your shovel sir and he's pissed at this like this is some clever thing I've said it's nothing. He has no idea what I am holding back from him right now and we go at it a little bit but I am calm the whole time in a way I can't stand. And so finally he goes to turn he's going to leave with the shovel and I recognize it's a $7 shovel from Target it's a good quality shovel though it's Charlie's best shovel and he's walking away with it. And so I say you know what you can walk away with the shovel I don't care but just know as you walk away you're a goddamn thief as you walk away and he turns and now he's pissed. And as he turns Charlie reaches up and reaches for the shovel and I say no Charlie don't and Charlie's not reaching for the shovel he says look. And on the underside of the shovel the man turns it turns it over on the underside it says Charlie and black Sharpie. My wife who puts my children's names on every single thing they own has put the name Charlie in ink on this shovel and I am so happy this thing that makes me crazy she puts it on spoons and on everything everything in my house as a kid's name on it. And now it's the most ingenious thing I've ever heard and I'm looking across the beach going yes honey you're an amazing person that is the best thing you have ever done and he just stares at me and he has nothing to say he tries to apologize and I say no go go find your shovel. And he just wanders away and I'm feeling terrible because there were so many terrible things I could have said to him to make him be sad or maybe even cry it would have been a beautiful thing but as he's walking away I see my wife and I think about how she tells me to be kinder to strangers and I've never really understood it.

## [06:09 - 07:19]

But the way Charlie just pointed up at his name. I start to think that maybe and I'm not sure I'm not saying this is true but maybe if I start going through life with Charlie at my feet right Charlie or proverbial Charlie. If I just start walking through life and maybe we all start walking through life with the Charlie at our feet. The words that we say to the strangers who we hate. They might not be as bad as they are right now. And so I do not always not often. But there are times when I am ready to make an old lady cry because she has cut me in line. That is not a lie. And I think about Charlie at my feet. I see him in my mind's eye. And the things that come out of my mouth are a little kinder and a little gentler and a little softer. I think my wife is probably right that the world will be better if we all have Charlie at our feet. Thank you. Thank you.
